children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “Federal land” means land the title to which is held by the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: a unit of the National Park System;

children.label: ii
children.text: a unit of the National Wildlife Refuge System;

children.label: iii
children.text: a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System;

children.label: iv
children.text: a wilderness study area within the National Forest System; or

children.label: v
children.text: Indian land.

children.heading: Gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit
children.label: 2 Gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit
i
children.text: a pipeline that is installed to transport oil, natural gas and related constituents, or produced water from 1 or more wells drilled and completed to produce oil or gas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if necessary, 1 or more compressors or pumps to raise the pressure of the transported oil, natural gas and related constituents, or produced water to higher pressures necessary to enable the oil, natural gas and related constituents, or produced water to flow into pipelines and other facilities.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit” includes a pipeline or associated compression or pumping unit that is installed to transport oil or natural gas from a processing plant to a common carrier pipeline or facility.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: C Exclusions
children.text: The term “gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit” does not include a common carrier pipeline.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States in trust for an Indian Tribe or an individual Indian; or

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian Tribe or an individual Indian subject to a restriction by the United States against alienation.

children.heading: Produced water
children.label: 4 Produced water
children.text: The term “produced water” means water produced from an oil or gas well bore that is not a fluid prepared at, or transported to, the well site to resolve a specific oil or gas well bore or reservoir condition.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 5 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: are within a field or unit for which an approved land use plan or an environmental document prepared pursuant to the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) analyzed transportation of oil, natural gas, or produced water from 1 or more oil or gas wells in the field or unit as a reasonably foreseeable activity;42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: i
children.text: any existing disturbed area; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an existing corridor for a right-of-way; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit transporting methane, the total quantity of methane that would otherwise be vented, flared, or unintentionally emitted from the field or unit; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a gathering line and associated field compression or pumping unit not transporting methane, the vehicular traffic that would otherwise service the field or unit.

children.label: A
children.text: to which the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) applies; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: for which the Indian Tribe with jurisdiction over the Indian land submits to the Secretary a written request that paragraph (1) apply to that Indian land (or portion of Indian land).

children.label: i
children.text: ; orsection 324 of title 25

children.label: ii
children.text: (commonly known as the “Indian Reorganization Act” );section 5123(e) of title 251See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: under ; orsection 306108 of title 54

children.label: C
children.text: under any other Federal law (including regulations) relating to Tribal consent for rights-of-way across Indian land; or
makes the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) applicable to land to which that Act otherwise would not apply.42 U.S.C. 4321
